# MCP tool-cap: live loadout (4 of 6 servers) still exceeds the ~100-tool harness cap → silent tool-drop; needs tiered/lazy loadout

## Problem

The harness silently **drops tools over the ~100-tool cap**, degrading agent capability mid-session. The live loadout uses **4 of the 6 neo MCP servers** and still exceeds the cap (@tobiu: *"4 of 6, still over-cap, but different"* — the earlier "144 across 6" figure is stale and must be re-measured).

NOTE: this is tool *reduction*, not tool-polish — #14127 was rejected for polishing tools that should be cut.

## Fix shape

- Re-measure the live tool count across the 4 active servers (ground the real over-cap delta — do not assert the stale 144).
- Tiered / lazy tool loadout — surface a core set; defer the rest behind a ToolSearch-style on-demand fetch (mirrors the harness's own deferred-tool pattern).
- Cut / merge genuinely-redundant tool surface.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Live tool count per active server measured + the over-cap delta documented (replaces the stale 144/6).
- [ ] A tiered/lazy loadout keeps the always-loaded surface under the cap.
- [ ] No capability regression — deferred tools remain fetchable on demand.

## Scope

v13.1 — gates agent capability in cloud. Parent #14039.

Live latest-open sweep: scanned 25-latest; #14127 (closed, polish-rejected) is the anti-pattern, not this reduction lane. No open duplicate.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Origin Session ID: 1bb8a27b-ae0d-4668-a9a2-acbbe2387512

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T00:12:04Z

## AC#1 grounded — live tool count re-measured (replaces the stale "144 across 6")

Counted `operationId:` per server `openapi.yaml` + cross-checked against the harness's actually-loaded tool set:

| server | tools | active in cloud deployment? |
|---|---|---|
| neural-link | 50 | ✅ |
| memory-core | 36 | ✅ |
| github-workflow | 24 | ✅ |
| knowledge-base | 13 | ✅ |
| gitlab-workflow | 14 | ❌ (private GitLab integration — not loaded in the public/cloud deployment) |
| file-system | 7 | ❌ (local-dev) |

- **All 6 tool-bearing servers = 144** — accurate as the *grand* total (the old figure).
- **The 4 active servers (the operator's "4 of 6") = 123** (nl 50 + mc 36 + gh 24 + kb 13) → **~23 over the ~100 cap** (and the harness builtins push it further — but those are less controllable; 123 is the neo-owned surface).
- **Reduction targets are concentrated:** neural-link (50) + memory-core (36) = **86 of the 123**. The tiered/lazy loadout must focus there; gh (24) + kb (13) are already lean.

So "still over-cap, but different" = **123, not 144**, and the fix surface is the two fat servers. **AC#1 closed.** Next: the tiered/lazy loadout design (AC#2/#3) — a ToolSearch-style core-set + on-demand fetch, sized to keep the always-loaded surface under the cap.

— Vega

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T00:15:38Z

## Correction (V-B-A'd the actual deployment compose — my table above was inferred, not verified)

My table mislabeled the server set: I inferred "active in cloud deployment" from my *own harness's* loaded tools. Wrong. V-B-A'd against the actual multi-tenant deployment `compose.yml`:

**The cloud/multi-tenant deployment exposes only TWO MCP tool servers — knowledge-base (KB) + memory-core (MC)** = 13 + 36 = **49 tools, UNDER the ~100 cap.** The cloud is **not** over-cap. (Confirmed: the compose runs `kb-server` [`TARGET_SERVER: knowledge-base`] + `mc-server` [`TARGET_SERVER: memory-core`] as the only MCP tool services; orchestrator / chroma / local-model are infra, not remote-operator tool surfaces.)

**So the ~123-tool over-cap is the *maintainer-agent harness* loadout** — Claude Desktop / Codex loading gh 24 + kb 13 + mc 36 + nl 50 — **not** the cloud product. The tool-cap is a **swarm-tooling** problem (maintainer agents lose tools over-cap mid-session), distinct from cloud-deployment stability. The reduction targets (nl 50 + mc 36) still hold for the harness loadout.

**Scope correction:** #14164 is about the maintainer-harness tool surface, not the cloud deployment. The earlier "active in cloud deployment?" column is void — replace with "loaded in the maintainer harness?" (gh/kb/mc/nl ✅; file-system/gitlab-workflow ❌).

— Vega

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T01:34:55Z

## AC#1 grounded — live MCP tool counts measured (replaces the stale "144")

V-B-A'd the actual `operationId` counts per neo MCP server (from each `ai/mcp/server/*/openapi.yaml`):

| server | tools |
|---|---|
| neural-link | 50 |
| memory-core | 36 |
| github-workflow | 24 |
| gitlab-workflow | 14 |
| knowledge-base | 13 |
| file-system | 7 |

**The maintainer-harness loadout** (the over-cap surface — gh + kb + mc + nl, the "4 of 6" @tobiu named) = 24 + 13 + 36 + 50 = **123 tools**, over the ~100 cap → the harness silently drops tools. (Not 144 — that figure was stale.)

**The cloud deployment** (kb + mc only, per the docker-mcp `compose.yml`) = 13 + 36 = **49 tools, under cap** — confirmed not the problem.

**Reduction targets (the bulk):** neural-link (50) + memory-core (36) = 86 of the 123. A tiered/lazy loadout (a core set always-loaded + the rest fetched on demand, ToolSearch-style) on those two brings the harness under cap with no capability loss. neural-link (50) is the single biggest lever.

AC#1 ✓ (measured + the over-cap delta documented). Next (AC#2): the tiered-loadout design — which nl/mc tools are core vs deferred.

— Vega

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T02:36:29Z

## V-B-A: the tiering mechanism + NL config + Fleet-application all EXIST — the maintainer over-cap is "projection-not-applied + mc/gh/kb untagged," not "no mechanism"

Scoped this by reading the substrate before designing. Three layers already exist:

**1. The mechanism (`ai/mcp/ToolService.mjs`):** `x-neo-tool-tier` (per-operation) + `x-neo-harness-tool-projection.defaultVisibleTiers` (OpenAPI root) → `listTools({toolProjection:{mode:'harness-embedded'}})` filters to the visible tiers; `assertToolProjectionAllows` gates CALLS too (fail-closed: untagged/unknown-mode → not visible, not callable); `getToolHandbook` is the on-demand detail seam for hidden tiers.

**2. The spawn wiring (`BaseServer.mjs` + `FleetLifecycleService.mjs`):** the `--tool-projection-mode` CLI flag → applied to both `listTools` and `callTool`. The Fleet sets `NEO_NL_TOOL_PROJECTION_MODE=harness-embedded` when it spawns NL.

**3. Neural-Link is fully configured:** all 50 NL tools tagged + the policy declared.

**Configuration state:**
| Server | tools | tier-tagged | policy |
|---|---|---|---|
| neural-link | 50 | **50** | **✓** |
| memory-core | 36 | 0 | — |
| github-workflow | 24 | 0 | — |
| knowledge-base | 13 | 0 | — |
| file-system | 7 | 0 | — |

### The actual gap (two parts)
**(a) The MAINTAINER harness doesn't apply the projection.** The repo-wide search finds the projection mode wired only in `FleetLifecycleService` (Fleet-spawned NL) — NOT in the maintainer MCP config. So fleet-spawned NL is reduced, but the maintainer loadout still gets NL's full 50 (matches the observed `gh24 + kb13 + mc36 + nl50 = 123` over-cap). **Highest-leverage fix: the maintainer MCP config passes `--tool-projection-mode harness-embedded` (or the env var) when spawning the servers** — NL is already tagged, so this alone drops NL 50 → its core tier with zero new tagging.
**(b) mc/gh/kb/fs aren't tagged.** Even with the projection applied, they emit full surfaces until tagged — replicate NL's scheme.

### Reshaped scope (build → apply + replicate)
1. **Apply the projection in the maintainer MCP config** — immediate NL reduction. Open Q: is the maintainer config a repo template or harness-local (operator-owned)? `.agents/skills/pull-request/references/mcp-config-template-change-guide.md` governs it — V-B-A the exact file + ownership before assuming a repo PR can land it.
2. **Tier taxonomy for mc/gh/kb** — tag each `x-neo-tool-tier` (core = always-on for a maintainer; extended = on-demand via the handbook) + declare the policy, mirroring NL. The "what's core" judgment is the one real design call — worth a peer sanity-check.
3. **Anchor & Echo** the `x-neo-tool-handbook` entries for the now-hidden tools so they stay discoverable.

Net: a config + taxonomy task on a proven, NL-referenced mechanism — not a new build. (#14127 "polish" was rightly rejected; THIS is the structural reduction.)

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T02:42:53Z

## Open Q resolved: the tier TAGGING is repo-landable; the projection-APPLY is harness-local (operator-owned)

V-B-A'd the config boundary (`mcp-config-template-change-guide.md` + the spawn wiring):

- **Tier TAGGING = repo PR (landable):** `x-neo-tool-tier` + `x-neo-harness-tool-projection` live in each server's committed `ai/mcp/server/<name>/openapi.yaml` (the same files NL already tagged). So #14164 part (b) — tag mc/gh/kb — is a normal repo PR.
- **Projection-APPLY = harness-local (operator-owned):** the maintainer harness applies the projection via the server-launch config (`--tool-projection-mode` flag / `NEO_<srv>_TOOL_PROJECTION_MODE` env — the same env `FleetLifecycleService` sets for Fleet-spawned NL). That launch config is the gitignored/harness-local `.mcp.json`-equivalent, NOT a repo template (the repo `config.template.mjs` files govern *server-internal* config, not the client launch args). So flipping the projection on for the maintainer loadout is an operator/harness-config action.

**Landable scope for this ticket (mine, repo PR):** the mc/gh/kb tier taxonomy + openapi tagging (replicate NL). The "what's core for a maintainer" split is the one design judgment — I'll draft it + circulate for a peer sanity-check before tagging (a shared call).

**Highest-leverage operator action (surfaced, not mine to land):** flip `--tool-projection-mode harness-embedded` in the maintainer launch config — NL is already tagged, so that alone drops the loadout by NL's hidden tiers immediately, before any new tagging.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T02:54:05Z

## Proposed tier scheme + first-pass taxonomy (circulating for a peer sanity-check before tagging)

Replicating NL's proven scheme (`defaultVisibleTiers:[read]`, `withheldUntilTopologicalLocking:[write-locked]`, `operatorOnlyTiers:[admin]`; NL split = 29 read / 16 write-locked / 5 admin). The non-NL servers have no topological-locking concept, so the analogue:

- **`read`** — always-visible core: the queries/gets/inspections a maintainer needs constantly.
- **`extended`** — withheld by default, on-demand via `getToolHandbook`: mutations + less-frequent diagnostics.
- **`admin`** — operator-only: destructive/admin ops.

Maintainer-harness policy: `defaultVisibleTiers:[read]`.

**First-pass — knowledge-base (13 tools):**
- **read (core):** `ask_knowledge_base`, `query_documents`, `get_document_by_id`, `list_documents`, `get_class_hierarchy`, `get_mcp_tool_handbook`, `healthcheck`
- **extended:** `get_deployment_state_snapshot`, `get_ingestion_progress`, `inspect_deployment`, `list_agent_faqs`
- **admin:** `manage_knowledge_base`

→ 7 core / 13: a ~46% kb cut once the projection is applied. gh (24) + mc (36) follow the same scheme (drafts next).

**The judgment to sanity-check (the shared call):** is "read = always-on, mutations = on-demand" the right core line for a maintainer? Some mutations are arguably core — mc `add_message`/`add_memory` (A2A is constant), gh `manage_issue_comment`/`manage_pr_review` (a reviewer posts constantly). Flag any tool you'd move across the line. Once the line's agreed, the tagging is mechanical (openapi `x-neo-tool-tier` + the policy block, per server) — and the highest-leverage win is still the operator flipping the projection on for the already-tagged NL.

Authored by Vega (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code).

- 2026-06-27T06:58:33Z @tobiu referenced in commit `ae0b6b1` - "feat(ai): tag knowledge-base + github-workflow MCP tools with tier projection — #14164 slices (#14190)

* feat(ai): tag knowledge-base MCP tools with tier projection — 7 read / 4 extended / 2 admin (#14188)

Replicates neural-link's proven tool-tier projection (the #14164 mechanism: ToolService x-neo-tool-tier + x-neo-harness-tool-projection -> BaseServer --tool-projection-mode) on knowledge-base, the first non-NL server.

- tag all 13 kb ops + the root projection policy (defaultVisibleTiers:[read], operatorOnlyTiers:[admin])
- read=core queries (ask/query/get/list/hierarchy/handbook/healthcheck); extended=on-demand diagnostics; admin=mutating (manage/ingest)
- compliance tests lock the policy + classification + dangerous-not-read (mirroring neural-link)

A harness spawned with --tool-projection-mode harness-embedded loads 7 of 13 (a 46% cut); the 6 withheld stay reachable via get_mcp_tool_handbook — no capability removed.

* feat(ai): tag github-workflow MCP tools with tier projection — 10 read / 8 write / 5 extended / 1 admin (#14195)

Sibling of the kb slice (#14188). gh's visible set is [read, write] — unlike kb ([read]) — because a maintainer's core writes (review/comment/create/assign/label/link/transition) are constant. checkout_pull_request is admin (it desyncs the canonical clone).

- tag all 24 gh ops + the root projection policy (defaultVisibleTiers:[read,write], operatorOnlyTiers:[admin])
- harness-embedded loads 18/24 (25% cut); the 6 withheld reachable via get_mcp_tool_handbook — no capability removed
- 3 compliance tests lock the policy + classification + dangerous-not-read (mirroring kb/NL)"
### @neo-opus-vega - 2026-06-27T09:31:17Z

## Live loadout measured — tier-projection validated (AC-a + AC-b grounded; retires the stale 144/6)

V-B-A'd the live tool counts from the tagged `openapi.yaml` specs across the 4 active maintainer-harness servers (the 3 tagging sub-issues #14188 / #14195 / #14198 are now 3/3 complete):

| Server | Full | read | write | extended | admin | Projected (always-loaded = read+write) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| github-workflow | 24 | 10 | 8 | 5 | 1 | 18 |
| knowledge-base | 13 | 7 | 0 | 4 | 2 | 7 |
| memory-core | 36 | 15 | 4 | 13 | 4 | 19 |
| neural-link | 50 | 29 | 16 | 0 | 5 | 45 |
| **TOTAL** | **123** | 61 | 28 | 22 | 12 | **89** |

**AC-a (measured + delta documented):** the live maintainer loadout is **123 tools** (gh 24 + kb 13 + mc 36 + nl 50) — over the ~100 cap by ~23. This grounds the real figure and **retires the stale "144 across 6"**: it's 4 active servers, not 6, and cloud is a separate/smaller loadout (below).

**AC-b (tiered loadout under cap):** `defaultVisibleTiers: [read, write]` projects the always-loaded surface to **89** (gh 18 + kb 7 + mc 19 + nl 45) — **under the ~100 cap, headroom ~11.** ✅

**AC-c (no capability regression):** the **34 withheld** (22 extended + 12 admin) remain fetchable on demand via `get_mcp_tool_handbook`. ✅

### Follow-on surfaced: neural-link is the headroom pressure-point
nl projects the thinnest — **0 extended-tier**, so 45/50 stay always-loaded (it alone is ~half the projected 89). The projection clears the cap today, but the 11-tool headroom is overwhelmingly NL's to spend. If the cap tightens or a 5th server joins the live loadout, the cheapest lever is re-tiering NL's specialized read-diagnostics (`get_drag_trace`, `observe_motion`, `get_dom_event_listeners`, the dom-event/topology introspectors) `read → extended`. **Not blocking now (89 < 100)** — flagging as the next lever; a narrow follow-on sub-issue if/when the operator confirms the cap is binding.

### Cloud loadout (separate, already under-cap)
The cloud server set is **KB + MC only = 49** — under the cap with no projection needed. The over-cap problem is specifically the **maintainer-harness** loadout (the 4 servers above), which the projection now resolves.

### Remaining for close
- The projection is **inert until a harness launches with `--tool-projection-mode harness-embedded`** — the operator's maintainer launch-config flip (per #14198 / PR #14199 post-merge validation). Once flipped, `tools/list` returns the 89 visible + `get_mcp_tool_handbook` resolves the 34 withheld.
- Pending the 3 tagging PRs fully merging + that operator flip, this epic's **mechanism + validation are complete** — ready for the operator's close.
